text: Ambiguity permeates the air and the room, as if every object and wall text is asking me to reconsider what I assume to be true. The glass reflects both myself and others—a distortion of presence and absence. The words on the wall speak about research, development, and connection, yet I remain suspended between observing and participating. The sense of faith, perhaps, is an openness to the unknown, an embrace of the uncertainty that defines this space. I feel a mix of curiosity and vulnerability, quietly challenged by the ambiguity all around me.
